key_findings: 200-yuan margin does not exonerate: AR-03 threshold is a hard boundary at ≥10,000, and the combined 10,200 crosses it by 200 (2%). No tolerance, rounding, or de minimis clause exists in the policy.

Article 11 trigger is fully satisfied: same employee (E0009), same expense type (travel_lodging), within 7 days (expense dates April 10 and April 15, 5 days apart), combined amount (10,200) reaches the AR-03 line (10,000).

Date overlap strongly indicates NOT genuinely separate stays: Assuming invoice dates represent checkout (hotel industry standard), first stay = April 3-10 (7 nights), second stay = April 8-15 (7 nights), overlapping April 8-10. One person cannot occupy two hotel rooms simultaneously.

Consecutive invoice numbers (FP2025X0004206, FP2025X0004207) from same vendor (安程酒店管理有限公司) with identical amounts (5,100 each), identical reason (项目现场住宿费), and identical city tier (A) are consistent with a single planned engagement split for approval purposes.
Approval tier avoidance is textbook: individual records at 5,100 each fall in AR-02 (3,000-9,999), but combined 10,200 falls in AR-03 (10,000-49,999), which requires 部门总经理 rather than just 部门经理.
Additional compliance issue: AR-02 requires both 部门经理 AND 财务复核 (per Authorization Annex II). Both records show only E0009 self-approval as 部门经理; no 财务复核 is present. E0009 approved her own expenses.
Per-night rates (728.57/night vs 850/night standard for D1/A类) are within policy regardless of single or split treatment, so rate compliance does not resolve the splitting question.
No special approval (special_approval=0), no专项审批, and no applicable exemption clause found for either record.
unresolved_items: Exact check-in/check-out dates are not directly recorded — the date overlap inference uses invoice dates as proxies for checkout dates. Direct stay-date evidence would strengthen the finding but is not required to sustain it under Article 11.
